meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome can't deploy apps. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Deployment Workflow to Solve Can't Deploy Apps
content.seo_description: Learn how to master your deployment workflow and overcome deployment failures. An advanced-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: Deploying apps fails when environment parity breaks and CI/CD pipelines lack automation. To solve this, you must adopt an infrastructure-as-code approach using Antigravity for environment provisioning and Claude Code for automated debugging. This systematic workflow ensures your local code mirrors production, eliminating 'it works on my machine' errors forever.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks perfecting your logic, polishing the UI, and squashing local bugs. You hit 'deploy,' expecting a live URL, only to be met with a cascade of cryptic error logs and 502 Bad Gateway screens. That moment of frustration is the rite of passage for every developer, but it’s a hurdle that separates hobbyists from professional engineers.

Deployment isn't magic; it’s a predictable engineering process. By treating your infrastructure as a repeatable, version-controlled artifact, you stop guessing and start shipping. This guide replaces the chaos of manual configuration with a high-velocity deployment workflow designed to get your portfolio project live and scalable.

steps.title: Environment Parity Audit
steps.description: Align your local development environment with your production cloud target to prevent configuration drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current local environment variables and project dependencies, then generate a Dockerfile and .env.example template that ensures parity with a standard production Linux environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit local environment against production requirements'

steps.title: Automated Infrastructure Provisioning
steps.description: Use Antigravity to spin up your cloud infrastructure as code, ensuring your deployment target is configured correctly before pushing code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an infrastructure manifest that provisions a scalable cloud environment for this web application, including database connectivity and secure secrets management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'provision infrastructure using antigravity manifest'

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration
steps.description: Automate the build, test, and deployment cycle so that every commit is vetted before reaching production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a CI/CD pipeline configuration that runs unit tests and static analysis before allowing any push to the production branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github actions for automated deployment'

steps.title: Automated Log Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude Code to parse deployment logs in real-time, instantly identifying the root cause of build failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Monitor the deployment output and identify any critical errors or missing dependencies in the build logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze deployment logs for errors'

steps.title: Health Check & Smoke Testing
steps.description: Verify your deployment with automated smoke tests that confirm the application is live and responsive.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of smoke tests that verify critical user flows like sign-up and data retrieval after a successful deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute smoke tests on production url'

pro_tips: Always use a .env.production file that is explicitly ignored by Git to keep secrets out of your repository.
Implement blue-green deployments to ensure zero downtime when you update your application.
Use ephemeral preview environments for every Pull Request so you can test features in a production-like setting before merging.
Set up automated uptime monitoring with alerts that ping your Slack or email the moment a service becomes unresponsive.
Treat your logs as primary documentation; if a bug occurs, the log is the only source of truth that matters.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on setting up domain mapping and SSL certification to give your project a professional, secure footprint.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the technical blueprint to deploy any application with confidence. Stop letting deployment errors bottleneck your progress and start building a portfolio that proves your engineering prowess. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use Antigravity to spin up your cloud infrastructure as code, ensuring your deployment target is configured correctly before pushing code.
Automate the build, test, and deployment cycle so that every commit is vetted before reaching production.
Use Claude Code to parse deployment logs in real-time, instantly identifying the root cause of build failures.
Verify your deployment with automated smoke tests that confirm the application is live and responsive.
